projects.title: LoadSense
projects.subtitle: Real information. Safer rides. Smarter cities.
projects.summary: Edge-AI + cloud occupancy platform for Philippine jeepneys, built with Team FlowerBoys for the ASEAN AI Hackathon 2026 Smart Cities track — Top 6 in track, Top 40 overall.
description: Commuters, operators, and regulators all operate blind on one variable that matters most: how full is the next jeepney. LoadSense turns every public utility vehicle into a real-time occupancy sensor, closing a gap that Google Maps and existing transit apps don't cover.

A YOLOv8-nano model runs fully offline on an overhead camera, performing bidirectional passenger counting and mapping the live count to a four-tier occupancy state shown on a windshield LED strip. A FastAPI + SQLite cloud layer ingests GPS and occupancy telemetry to predict ETA, forecast demand, detect route deviations and driving anomalies, and power an operator console with a live fleet map and an alert verification workflow.
highlightList: Bidirectional passenger counting with an offline YOLOv8-nano model
Four-tier occupancy state reflected on a windshield LED strip and live map
ETA prediction, demand forecasting, and route-deviation/anomaly alerts on the cloud layer
Operator console with an ack → verify → escalate alert workflow, not black-box auto-escalation

projects.title: Hira
projects.subtitle: Persona-aware labor platform for the Philippines
projects.summary: A persona-aware Filipino labor-matching platform for blue-collar workers, fresh graduates, and OFWs — built as HCI coursework and drafted into an independent Design Science Research paper.
description: Hira matches distinct personas — blue-collar workers, fresh graduates, returning OFWs, employers, and government agencies — against tailored flows.
The current build is a frontend-only demo running entirely on mock data, styled with a forest-green design system and built in Next.js 16 + Tailwind v4 + TypeScript.
highlightList: Persona-driven flows for job seekers, employers, and government agencies
Full Figma design build alongside the coded frontend
Independent Design Science Research paper drafted on the platform's design approach
Frontend demo requires no backend — runs on mock data

projects.title: Email Security Classifier
projects.subtitle: Naive Bayes fraud & prompt-injection detection
projects.summary: A Naive Bayes classifier built for an Intelligent Systems final that flags fraudulent/phishing emails and prompt-injection attempts, backed by a custom labeled dataset.
description: Built a custom 300-row CSV dataset spanning three classes — safe, fraudulent, and prompt-injection emails — then built text-feature extraction and a Naive Bayes classifier around it.
Wrapped the model in a Streamlit app so the classifier could be demonstrated live during the course defense.
highlightList: Custom 300-row labeled dataset across three email classes
Naive Bayes classification with hand-built text-feature extraction
Streamlit demo app for the finals defense

projects.title: Discord Music Bot
projects.subtitle: Slash-command music bot for YouTube & Spotify
projects.summary: A personal, fully slash-command Discord music bot with YouTube and Spotify playback, queue management, lyrics, and local caching for 24/7 hosting.
description: Supports multi-source playback (YouTube, Spotify tracks/playlists/albums), full queue controls, lyrics lookup, and clean embeds — no legacy prefix commands.
Built for always-on hosting: local audio caching with automatic cleanup, rotating logs with 7-day retention, and systemd/Task-Scheduler-friendly deployment.
highlightList: Multi-source playback: YouTube and Spotify, including playlists and albums
Local audio caching with automatic cleanup for 24/7 hosting
